markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' confirmed November 19, 2026 release date for Grand Theft Auto VI, following multiple delays from a 2025 target to May and then November 2026 for extra polish, sets the core timeline traders monitor. Take-Two Interactive has repeatedly affirmed the game remains on track during earnings updates, with no further postponements signaled, though the studio's history of prioritizing quality over rushed launches fuels speculation around additional trailers or marketing pushes in the coming months. This extended window amplifies interest in unrelated cultural or global events resolving beforehand, as the market reflects trader views on developments before launch.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrial declarations in Harvey Weinstein’s New York rape retrials, including the mid-May 2026 deadlock on charges stemming from a 2013 incident, combined with ongoing appeals of his 2022 California conviction carrying a 16-year term, underpin the heavy market weighting toward no additional prison time. Weinstein, currently held at Rikers Island due to the California sentence, has cited serious health complications including cancer and heart issues that could influence sentencing or release considerations. Traders appear to factor in the high bar for new convictions after multiple mistrials, historical appeal patterns in similar cases, and the consecutive nature of any potential sentences. Key upcoming catalysts include California appellate rulings and any decision on scheduling another New York proceeding.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes enter the 2026 Stanley Cup Final as slight favorites following their 12-1 playoff mark, including a 4-1 Eastern Conference Final series win capped by a 6-1 Game 5 victory over the Montreal Canadiens. Their league-leading 53-22-7 regular-season record and +56 goal differential reflect consistent depth and goaltending stability from Frederik Andersen. The Vegas Golden Knights counter with a 12-4 postseason record, highlighted by a four-game Western Conference Final sweep of the Colorado Avalanche, plus contributions from Mitch Marner and improved netminding. Traders appear to weigh Carolina’s superior regular-season standing and momentum edge against Vegas’s proven resilience in reaching its third Final since 2017.

events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks' sweep of the Cleveland Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference Finals has positioned them as the Eastern representative in the 2026 NBA Finals for the first time since 1999, boosting their implied probability in the title market. In the West, the Oklahoma City Thunder enter Game 7 of the conference finals against the San Antonio Spurs holding a narrow series edge despite recent losses, buoyed by their status as defending champions, league-best regular-season record, and core of Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Jalen Williams, and Chet Holmgren. The Spurs have narrowed the gap through Victor Wembanyama's dominant playoff performances and roster additions like De'Aaron Fox, reflecting trader reassessment of the competitive Western matchup and its impact on overall title odds.

markets.question: Will the New York Knicks win the 2026 NBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the New York Knicks win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain edges France as the narrow leader in the 2026 World Cup winner market thanks to La Roja’s status as European champions and a deep squad featuring Lamine Yamal, whose potential return from a hamstring injury has stabilized their implied probability near 17 percent. France sits just behind, buoyed by longstanding depth and Didier Deschamps’ experience, while England benefits from back-to-back major final appearances. Brazil and Argentina maintain strong positioning as South American powers with proven tournament pedigree, and Portugal rounds out the tightly bunched group. This competitive balance stems from multiple sides possessing elite talent, solid recent form, and no single dominant narrative, keeping the top probabilities clustered within a few percentage points.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s continued dominance within the Chinese Communist Party, reinforced by ongoing personnel reshuffles and anti-corruption campaigns targeting military and civilian elites, underpins the strong trader consensus that he will remain in power through the end of 2026. Recent leadership adjustments, including early 2026 purges of senior PLA officials and broader five-yearly post rotations, have centralized authority without visible elite challenges or an anointed successor. Official activities such as directing the start of the 15th Five-Year Plan, high-level diplomatic engagements, and public emphasis on party self-governance and stability further signal institutional continuity ahead of the 21st Party Congress in late 2027. These developments leave little room for removal within the market’s resolution window, consistent with the 92.5% implied probability on “No.”

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 25.1% in the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential field, reflecting his California governorship, term-limited status, and recent national visibility through public exchanges with the Trump administration alongside a populist shift on issues like AI regulation. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 10.4% on the strength of progressive support and early outreach including a national tour and Southern voter engagement, while Kamala Harris holds 8.0% tied to prior national exposure. Lower odds for figures such as Pete Buttigieg, Josh Shapiro, and Jon Ossoff reflect regional bases and policy records that have yet to consolidate broader backing. Recent polling shows modest shifts among top names, and the November 2026 midterms plus subsequent endorsements and primary performance could narrow the fragmented field by elevating one contender.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 25.1% in the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential field, reflecting his California governorship, term-limited status, and recent national visibility through public exchanges with the Trump administration alongside a populist shift on issues like AI regulation. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 10.4% on the strength of progressive support and early outreach including a national tour and Southern voter engagement, while Kamala Harris holds 8.0% tied to prior national exposure. Lower odds for figures such as Pete Buttigieg, Josh Shapiro, and Jon Ossoff reflect regional bases and policy records that have yet to consolidate broader backing. Recent polling shows modest shifts among top names, and the November 2026 midterms plus subsequent endorsements and primary performance could narrow the fragmented field by elevating one contender.
